title: Urophyllum annamense
taxonomy: Urophyllum annamense belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the madder family and includes over 600 genera. Urophyllum annamense is a species of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. It is a type of shrub or small tree that produces small, white or yellowish flowers. The classification of Urophyllum annamense is further divided into the subfamily Rubioideae and the tribe Urophylleae.
uses: Urophyllum annamense has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Vietnam, for its medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional Vietnamese medicine, Urophyllum annamense is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential applications. Further studies on the plant's chemical composition and biological activities are required to confirm its efficacy and safety.
